Title: Inositol hexakisphosphate biosynthesis underpins PAMP-triggered immunity to Pseudomonas syringae pv. tomato in Arabidopsis thaliana but is dispensable for establishment of systemic acquired resistance

Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Catalyses conversion of glucose-6-phosphate to inositol phosphate, the first step in InsP6 biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: phosphate storage and signalling; supports effective basal resistance to pathogens
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Loss of IPS2 increases susceptibility to virulent and avirulent Pseudomonas syringae pv. tomato and compromises basal resistance, but does not impair flg22-induced local acquired resistance or SAR
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. ips2 mutant plants were hypersusceptible to virulent and avirulent Pseudomonas syringae pv. tomato. 2. ips2/3 double mutants were also hypersusceptible, supporting a role for IPS2 in basal resistance linked to InsP6 biosynthesis. 3. ips2 mutants still exhibited flg22-induced local acquired resistance and systemic acquired resistance was not blocked in InsP6 biosynthetic mutants.
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Previously reported as vascular- and hydathode-specific in Arabidopsis
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Previously reported as vascular- and hydathode-specific in Arabidopsis
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: T-DNA loss-of-function mutant analysis; RT-qPCR; pathogen infection assay with virulent and avirulent Pst; flg22-induced resistance assay; SAR assay
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: flg22 treatment; infection with virulent Pseudomonas syringae pv. tomato DC3000 or avirulent Pst AvrB; wounding/water infiltration
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Mutation causes pathogen hypersusceptibility; no impairment reported for flg22-induced local acquired resistance or SAR
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Avoid broad suppression of InsP6 biosynthesis in crops because reduced InsP6 can compromise pathogen basal defence; if engineering low-phytate crops, restrict reduction to edible tissues
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: IPS2 expression was enhanced in ips1 and ips3 mutants, indicating regulatory cross-talk among IPS genes; authors infer IPS2 and IPS3 may contribute to distinct InsP6 pools for defence.

Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Catalyses conversion of glucose-6-phosphate to inositol phosphate, the first step in InsP6 biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: phosphate storage and signalling; supports effective basal resistance to pathogens
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Knockdown/loss of IPS3 increases susceptibility to virulent and avirulent Pseudomonas syringae pv. tomato and compromises basal resistance, but does not impair flg22-induced local acquired resistance or SAR
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. The ips3 T-DNA mutant was validated as a loss-of-function line by RT-qPCR. 2. ips3 plants were significantly more susceptible to both virulent and avirulent Pseudomonas syringae pv. tomato. 3. ips3 and ips2/3 mutants retained flg22-induced local acquired resistance, indicating IPS3 is required for basal resistance but not this PTI output.
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Previously reported as vascular- and hydathode-specific in Arabidopsis
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Previously reported as vascular- and hydathode-specific in Arabidopsis
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: T-DNA mutant identification and genotyping; RT-qPCR; pathogen infection assay with virulent and avirulent Pst; flg22-induced resistance assay; SAR assay
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: flg22 treatment; infection with virulent Pseudomonas syringae pv. tomato DC3000 or avirulent Pst AvrB; wounding/water infiltration
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Mutation causes pathogen hypersusceptibility; no impairment reported for flg22-induced local acquired resistance or SAR
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Avoid reducing IPS3-dependent InsP6 biosynthesis broadly in crops because it may weaken basal pathogen defence
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: IPS3 expression was enhanced in ips2 mutants, indicating regulatory cross-talk with IPS2; authors suggest IPS2 and IPS3 generate distinct defence-relevant InsP6 pools.

Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Catalyses the final phosphorylation step converting inositol-(1,3,4,5,6)-pentakisphosphate to InsP6
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: key control point for PTI-related InsP6 requirement inferred from mutant phenotype; formal rate-limiting status not established
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: phosphate storage and signalling; required for effective basal resistance and flg22-induced local acquired resistance
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Mutation of IPK1 increases susceptibility to Pseudomonas syringae pv. tomato and abolishes flg22-induced local acquired resistance, but does not block SAR, flg22-induced marker gene expression, Ca2+ influx, oxidative burst, or root growth inhibition
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. ipk1 mutants were hypersusceptible to virulent and avirulent Pseudomonas syringae pv. tomato, showing compromised basal resistance. 2. ipk1 mutants failed to exhibit flg22-induced local acquired resistance, unlike ips mutants. 3. Despite this, ipk1 mutants retained flg22-induced Ca2+ influx, oxidative burst, root growth inhibition, and induction of MPK3, MPK11, CAD5, and FRK1, and they still established SAR.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Loss-of-function mutant analysis; pathogen infection assay; flg22 pretreatment resistance assay; RT-qPCR of PTI marker genes; aequorin-based Ca2+ influx assay; luminol oxidative burst assay; seedling root growth inhibition assay; SAR assay
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: flg22 treatment; infection with virulent Pseudomonas syringae pv. tomato DC3000 or avirulent Pst AvrB; wounding/water infiltration
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Mutation causes pathogen hypersusceptibility and loss of flg22-induced resistance, although other flg22 responses remain intact
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Strong caution for low-phytate engineering: reducing IPK1 activity may compromise basal and PAMP-triggered immunity; tissue-specific rather than whole-plant reduction of InsP6 is preferable
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: ipk1 phenotype separates flg22-induced resistance from several canonical early PTI outputs, suggesting InsP6 biosynthesis is required for a specific downstream defence branch rather than all flg22 responses.
